title: Issue
description: Validates an issue artifact — the published newspaper issue. Grounded facts, declared uncertainties, omissions, and archive gaps are each distinguishable. Produced by publish; terminal.

published_stories.description: The final stories as published — development stories and archive-gap stories. Every claim carries a source; no claim grounding marked unsupported appears.

coverage_report.description: Committed-versus-published accounting, including which stories report developments and which report archive gaps.

publication_target.description: Where this issue was emitted — echoes brief.publication_target.

candidate_id.description: The candidate this story reports — traces to lineup and dispatch.

kind.description: development = a reported development. archive_gap = a reported materially relevant silence in the record. A reader can distinguish the two.

qualified_claims.description: The story's claims that were published as qualified rather than firm — surfaced here so a reader can see, per story, which assertions are hedged and what their stated limits are. Empty or absent when every claim in the story is firm.

claim_id.description: The draft claim this entry corresponds to.

limits.description: What the source does not establish — the declared limit of the claim.

committed.description: The candidate_ids the lineup selected to run.

published.description: The candidate_ids actually published in this issue.

omitted.description: Candidates not run, carried from the lineup.

unresolved.description: Candidates committed but not published. Should be empty; a non-empty list is the coverage red signal.

archive_gaps_reported.description: The candidate_ids of published stories whose kind is archive_gap — the issue's explicit account of where the record was found wanting.

disposition.description: deferred = a live thread for a future issue; dropped = not pursued further.

thread_id.description: When deferred, the thread id under which it carries forward.
